mappings.rationale: A core text on Islam encompassing its history, Sunnism, Shiism, Islamic law, the Ottoman Empire, and the prophet, the primary subject of 828.
Directly relevant to 828.B.1, examining the Quran's form, contents, revelation in Mecca and Medina, and its diverse interpretations.
Traces the life and teachings of the Prophet Muhammad including Abu Bakr and the early Muslim community, directly relevant to 828.A.2.
Surveys Islamic history from its origins through the Ottoman Empire including Hadith, jihad, and Mecca, the subject of 828.A.
Examines Shariah, Islamic jurisprudence, family law, and penal law, directly relevant to 828.B.3 and 828.C.3.d.
Traces Islam's worldwide development, the role of communication technologies, and its contemporary evolution, relevant to 828.A.6-7 on Islam in the modern world.
Explores Islamic philosophy including Avicenna, Mulla Sadra, and the role of reason in Islam, directly relevant to 828.B.4.
Avicenna stands as one of the most important Islamic philosophers, making this text essential for 828.B.4 on major schools of Islamic philosophy.
Islamic fundamentalism as a modern religious phenomenon is central to understanding 828.A.6 on Islam in the modern world.
Explores Islamic mystical traditions essential for understanding Sufism, the subject of 828.B.5.
The hajj to Mecca, one of the Five Pillars listed in 828.C.3.a, is a central topic of this study of pilgrimage.
Explores concepts of God including Islamic monotheism, relevant to 828.C.1.a on doctrines concerning God.
Places Islam in comparative perspective with Judaism and Christianity, illuminating Islam's Abrahamic context.
Theological approaches to doctrines of creation and prayer inform understanding of Islamic theology in 828.B.4 and 828.C.1.
Iran as a major Shia Muslim country and the site of the Islamic revolution is directly relevant to 828.C.2.d on Shiism.
The Crusades represent a defining encounter between Islam and Christianity, relevant to 828.A.5 on the Middle Ages of Islam.
Addresses the conflict including the Dome of the Rock and Jerusalem's significance in Islam, relevant to 828.A.7 on Islam today.
Examines the relationship between religion and warfare including concepts of jihad and just war, relevant to 828.A on Islam's military expansion.
Interpretive theory is foundational to Quranic interpretation and Islamic hermeneutics, relevant to 828.B.1.
Addresses blasphemy laws in Islamic contexts and the anti-iconic principle, relevant to 828.C.4.c.
Explores debates about secularism in Islamic societies and church-state relations, relevant to 828.A.6.
The Silk Road facilitated the spread of Islam across Central Asia, relevant to 828.A.3-4.
Pakistan as a major Islamic nation created through partition is directly relevant to 828.A.7 on Islam today.
Islam is a major religion across Southeast Asia, making this text relevant to 828.A.3-4 on the expansion of Islam.
Traces the spread of Islam in Africa, relevant to 828.A.3-4 on the expansion of Islam beyond Arabia.
Byzantine interactions with Islam and the fall of Constantinople are essential context for 828.A.4-5.
Ritual practices including the five daily prayers and festivals are central to Islamic worship, relevant to 828.C.3.a-b.
